description: An assertion gives false confidence because it passed vacuously, its precondition never occurring during simulation.
body: Failure mode: a concurrent assertion's antecedent is never satisfied by the stimulus, so the assertion reports passing without ever evaluating its real check. Detection signals: an assertion that never fails across all tests, zero coverage on its precondition, and the rule it guards still violated in hardware. Do not count a vacuously passing assertion as evidence. Safer fallback: add coverage on the antecedent, drive stimulus that triggers the precondition, and inject a fault to confirm the assertion can actually fire.
